description.task: Create an InputSanitizer class that protects against common injection attacks. Implement sanitization for SQL injection (parameterized-query safe values), XSS (HTML entity encoding), and command injection (shell metacharacter removal). Return whether input is safe after sanitization.
instructions: sanitizeForHtml() must replace all 5 special characters with their HTML entity equivalents
The '&' replacement must happen FIRST to avoid double-encoding
isSafeForSql() returns false if any of the 4 SQL injection indicators are present (case-insensitive)
sanitizeForShell() strips all specified metacharacters using a regex character class
All methods must handle null input gracefully (return empty string or true as appropriate)
description.hint: Fix the sanitizeForHtml() method — the stubs use HTML entities without the proper escaping (e.g., '<' should be the literal string '<'). Check the replace calls carefully. For sanitizeForShell(), the regex character class needs proper Java escaping.

description.task: Implement a DataMasker class that prevents sensitive data exposure in logs, responses, and error messages. Mask credit card numbers, email addresses, and API keys in strings, replacing middle characters with asterisks while preserving enough context to identify the data type.
instructions: maskCreditCard() strips spaces and dashes, then formats as 'XXXX-****-****-XXXX' using first and last 4 digits
maskEmail() keeps the first 2 characters of the local part, replaces the rest with '***', and preserves the full domain
maskApiKey() uses a regex to find 'apikey=...' patterns and masks everything after the first 4 key characters
All methods return the original value unchanged if it does not match the expected format
All methods must handle null input without throwing exceptions
description.hint: maskApiKey() uses capturing groups in replaceAll: group 1 is 'apikey=', group 2 is first 4 chars ([A-Za-z0-9]{4}), group 3 matches the rest. Replacement is '$1$2****'.

description.task: Build a SecurityAuditLogger class that records security-relevant events (login attempts, permission denials, data access) for compliance and monitoring. Implement filtering by event type and time range, and produce an audit summary report.
instructions: log() creates and appends an AuditEvent to the events list
filterByType() returns all events matching the given EventType
filterByTimeRange() returns events where timestampMs is between fromMs and toMs (both inclusive)
summarize() returns a map of EventType to the count of matching events
All methods work correctly on an empty events list (return empty collections/maps)
description.hint: filterByTimeRange() uses stream filter with e.timestampMs() >= fromMs && e.timestampMs() <= toMs. For summarize(), use Collectors.groupingBy(AuditEvent::type, Collectors.counting()).
